CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-26-2023 Continued To Dec-20-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Compel Binding Arbitration
Off Calendar Sep-29-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA Motion To Compel Binding Arbitration.

Denied.

The motion to compel arbitration is brought by defendant car manufacturer, Hyundai Motor America (HMA). It is premised on two documents: the warranty which accompanied the lease of the car to plaintiff and the agreement plaintiff signed with the dealer, but not with HMA.

As to the warranty: (1) There is no evidence plaintiff agreed to the arbitration provisions associated with the warranty. Indeed, there is no evidence plaintiff was made aware of the arbitration agreement in connection with the warranty. (2) Plaintiff's claims do not invoke the terms of the warranty document. All claims in the complaint invoke the Song-Beverly Consumer Warranty Act. As plaintiff puts it in the Opposition at 5, "Plaintiff's complaint in no way alleges that Plaintiff ever relied on HMA's Warranty Booklet to bringing forward claims under the Act."

As to the dealer agreement, HMA relies on Felisilda v. FCA US LC (2020) 53 Cal.App.5th 486 in an effort to secure the benefits of an arbitration agreement to which HMA is not a party. This court will not follow Felisilda and does follow Ford Motor Warranty Cases (2023) 89 Cal.App.5th 1324 (review granted, S279969); Montemayor v. Ford Motor Co. (2023) 92 Cal.App.5th 958; Ngo v. BMW of North America, LLC (9th Cir. 2022) 23 F.4th 942; Kielar v. Superior Court (Aug. 16, 2023) 2023 WL 5270559; Jaquelyn Yeh v. Superior Court (Sept. 6, 2023) 2023 WL 5741703. For the reasons discussed in those cases, equitable estoppel is inappropriate here.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Curtis Karnow, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/CK)
